source_jobs.title: Account Manager
source_jobs.description:
Proxima is the Conversion Intelligence platform for ecommerce. Powered by a proprietary Commerce Graph spanning 73M+ buyers across 2,000+ ecommerce brands, we help teams understand what actually drives conversion across the broader market, and turn those signals into clear creative direction.

Our data is deterministic (real names, emails, and addresses from actual orders, not inferred from sessions), granular (full SKU-level receipt data), and point-in-time (timestamped history that enables recency scoring and ML without look-ahead bias). Headquartered in New York City, we're a team of operators, builders, and ecommerce veterans solving the hardest problems in modern customer acquisition.


ABOUT PROXIMA

Most companies spend years trying to understand their customers. We already know them. Proxima is a data intelligence platform built on a proprietary, real-time database of over 70 million anonymized shopper personas and growing. We give B2C businesses the AI-powered intelligence they need to acquire customers profitably, retain them efficiently, and operate smarter at scale.

We're on a mission to put institutional-grade data intelligence within reach of every brand — not just the giants. Profitable growth starts with better data, and we've built the engine to prove it.



About The Role

As an Account Manager at Proxima, you will be responsible for ensuring our clients achieve and maximize their ROI through outstanding support and strategic guidance. The ideal candidate brings extensive experience in account management, excelling at client retention, contract renewals, and revenue expansion. You will leverage your expertise in data analysis and Paid Social best practices to drive measurable performance against client goals.

You’ll develop and implement account strategies with a sharp commercial acumen and an in-depth understanding of the Paid Social advertising landscape. Partnering closely with Sales and Media Buying teams, you’ll identify opportunities for growth and expansion within existing accounts. If you thrive in fast-changing environments, have a knack for project management, and enjoy collaborating across functions, this is the role for you.



What You'll Do

 - Build and manage strong client relationships, balancing Paid Social advertising performance and quarterly revenue objectives

 - Own the commercial relationship with clients, prioritizing contract renewals, client retention, and revenue growth

 - Develop and execute targeted audience building strategies to increase both the quality and quantity of high-intent leads

 - Analyze and interpret data and metrics to assess performance and adjust strategies as needed

 - Collaborate with Media Buying and Sales teams to create comprehensive campaign strategies across acquisition, retention, and remarketing goals

 - Track the success of campaigns using metrics such as CPA, CAC, ROI, and ROAS

 - Identify and pursue opportunities for growth and expansion within existing client portfolios

 - Deliver regular reporting and updates to clients on campaign performance and ROI

 - Act as a trusted advisor, helping clients achieve their business goals

 - Manage and lead projects, collaborating with cross-functional teams, and maintain exceptional, supportive client relationships
   
   

What We're Looking For

 - 5+ years of experience in digital marketing, with a focus on Paid Media and analytics

 - Previous experience in a Marketing/Media Agency, Social Publisher, or as a Social Marketing Partner

 - Strong knowledge of Google and Meta analytics, including hands-on experience with Meta Ads Manager and Meta Analytics; experience with additional social publishers is a plus

 - Advanced analytical skills with the ability to translate data into actionable client insights

 - Demonstrated success in managing client relationships and driving business growth through digital marketing solutions

 - Excellent interpersonal skills and client management experience

 - Proce

source_jobs.title: Senior Account Executive, Mobile Gaming and Apps
source_jobs.description:
Proxima is the Conversion Intelligence platform for ecommerce. Powered by a proprietary Commerce Graph spanning 73M+ buyers across 2,000+ ecommerce brands, we help teams understand what actually drives conversion across the broader market, and turn those signals into clear creative direction.

Our data is deterministic (real names, emails, and addresses from actual orders, not inferred from sessions), granular (full SKU-level receipt data), and point-in-time (timestamped history that enables recency scoring and ML without look-ahead bias). Headquartered in New York City, we're a team of operators, builders, and ecommerce veterans solving the hardest problems in modern customer acquisition.


ABOUT THE ROLE

Proxima is launching a mobile-focused vertical, and we're hiring the inaugural Senior Account Executive to build it. In this foundational role, you'll own outbound into mobile gaming studios, social casinos, and app developers, working alongside User Acquisition and growth leaders to show how Proxima's Commerce Graph and targeting tools improve campaign performance and scale acquisition. You'll define the go-to-market playbook, refine the sales motion, and shape the target account strategy as we build out this team.

What You'll Help Build

 - Build the go-to-market foundation for Proxima's mobile vertical — including outbound strategy, positioning, and sales process

 - Scale Proxima's mobile advertising business across mobile gaming studios, social casinos, and app-based advertisers focused on user acquisition and growth

 - Drive revenue through custom audiences and targeting solutions designed to help advertisers acquire high-value users more efficiently across digital channels

 - Act as a consultative partner to UA, growth, and marketing teams — advising on audience strategy, targeting approaches, and customer acquisition initiatives

 - Help gaming and app advertisers leverage proprietary audience data to improve campaign performance, scale growth, and reach high-value users

Who We're Looking For

 - 5+ years of quota-carrying sales experience in ad tech, mobile advertising, gaming, performance marketing, or SaaS

 - Track record of building pipeline and closing revenue in a new or developing market

 - Existing network of contacts within mobile gaming publishers, app advertisers, or UA/growth teams strongly preferred

 - Deep interest in mobile gaming, social casino, app growth, and user acquisition

 - Comfortable operating in a fast-paced, early-stage environment and excited to help build a business from the ground up

 - Strong communication and relationship-building skills with the ability to engage growth, UA, and marketing stakeholders

 - Motivated by outbound prospecting, pipeline generation, and helping shape the early sales motion for a growing mobile-focused team



Why Proxima

 - Work on one of the most differentiated data assets in consumer tech

 - High ownership and accountability — drive projects end-to-end and make decisions that actually matter

 - Lean team where your contributions are visible and your voice carries weight

 - Highly cross-functional — work closely with product, data, growth, and leadership

 - Hybrid setup in our NYC office with a collaborative in-person culture, plus quarterly team events in and out of the office

Benefits & Perks

 - Competitive base salary and meaningful equity

 - Comprehensive medical, dental, and vision coverage

 - HSA and FSA options

 - 401(k)

 - 12 weeks of fully paid parental leave

 - Unlimited PTO

 - Summer Fridays from July 4th through Labor Day

 - Mental health and wellness support through Talkspace, Health Advocate, and One Medical

 - Commuter benefits

 - $1,000 annual learning & development stipend

 - Quarterly team events, both in and out of the office

How to Apply

Submit your application through our Ashby job board. We review every application and will reach out directly if there's a potential fit.

Proxima is an equal opportunity emp

source_jobs.title: Senior Fullstack Engineer (Backend Focus)
source_jobs.description:
Proxima is the Conversion Intelligence platform for ecommerce. Powered by a proprietary Commerce Graph spanning 73M+ buyers across 2,000+ ecommerce brands, we help teams understand what actually drives conversion across the broader market, and turn those signals into clear creative direction.

Our data is deterministic (real names, emails, and addresses from actual orders, not inferred from sessions), granular (full SKU-level receipt data), and point-in-time (timestamped history that enables recency scoring and ML without look-ahead bias). Headquartered in New York City, we're a team of operators, builders, and ecommerce veterans solving the hardest problems in modern customer acquisition.

About the Role

We're looking for a Senior Full Stack Engineer to help build the AI-driven products at the core of our platform. You'll work directly with our proprietary dataset — real-time, anonymized cross-brand transaction data — to build the services, agents, and tools that power Conversion Intelligence.

This is a full stack role with a center of gravity in the backend. The majority of your work will be backend services, data systems, AI orchestration, and APIs — but you'll also own the UIs that surface this work to users, so you should be genuinely comfortable across the stack. If you thrive in fast-moving environments and are energized by pushing the limits of AI-assisted development, we'd love to talk.

What You'll Own

 - Design, build, and document backend services and APIs that power our AI-driven products

 - Develop end-to-end features across AI agent interfaces, orchestration layers, and developer-facing tools

 - Query, model, and manage relational databases — own the data layer, not just consume it

 - Build and scale the systems that turn cross-brand transaction data into product

 - Ship responsive, clean UIs in TypeScript and React where the product needs them

 - Own projects from architecture to deployment in an agile, high-velocity environment

 - Collaborate cross-functionally to ship high-impact features quickly

 - Stay current on modern AI development practices and tooling

Qualifications

 - 6+ years of professional software development experience

 - Strong backend proficiency in TypeScript, with solid working knowledge of SQL and relational database design

 - Demonstrated experience building or integrating AI-powered applications — agents, orchestration, or LLM-backed systems

 - Proven track record in startup environments — you've shipped fast and worn multiple hats

 - Comfortable owning projects independently in high-velocity environments

 - Working proficiency in React and the ability to build production UIs when the work calls for it

 - Daily use of AI-assisted coding tools (Cursor, GitHub Copilot, Claude Code, etc.) — this is a requirement, not a nice-to-have

 - Bachelor's degree or higher in Computer Science, Engineering, or a related field

Nice to Have

 - Proficiency in Python

 - Experience with AI agent frameworks, orchestration tools, or embeddings

 - Background in data engineering (ETL, dbt, Airflow, etc.)

 - Familiarity with cloud platforms (AWS, GCP, Azure) and containerization (Docker, Kubernetes)

 - Experience writing production-grade CSS/SCSS



Location

Open to remote candidates based in the U.S. or hybrid candidates in New York City, with a preference for NYC.



Work Authorization: Must be authorized to work in the U.S. without sponsorship. Visa transfers may be considered on a case-by-case basis.



Why Proxima

 - Work on one of the most differentiated data assets in consumer tech

 - High ownership and accountability — drive projects end-to-end and make decisions that actually matter

 - Lean team where your contributions are visible and your voice carries weight

 - Highly cross-functional — work closely with product, data, growth, and leadership

 - Hybrid setup in our NYC office with a collaborative in-person culture, plus quarterly team events in and out of the office

Benef
